title: SUBSCRIBE
description: Listens for messages published to channels.
complexity: O(N) where N is the number of channels to subscribe to.

jedis.signature: void subscribe(BinaryJedisPubSub jedisPubSub, final byte[]... channels)
void subscribe(final JedisPubSub jedisPubSub, final String... channels)

content:
Subscribes the client to the specified channels. Once the client enters the subscribed state it is not supposed to issue any other commands, except for additional `SUBSCRIBE`, [`SSUBSCRIBE`](), [`PSUBSCRIBE`](), [`UNSUBSCRIBE`](), [`SUNSUBSCRIBE`](), [`PUNSUBSCRIBE`](), [`PING`](), [`RESET`]() and [`QUIT`]() commands. However, if RESP3 is used (see [`HELLO`]()) it is possible for a client to issue any commands while in subscribed state. For more information, see [Pub/sub](). ## Behavior change history * `>= 6.2.0`: [`RESET`]({{< relref "/commands/reset" >}}) can be called to exit subscribed state. ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="subscribe-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} When successful, this command doesn't return anything. Instead, for each channel, one message with the first element being the string `subscribe` is pushed as a confirmation that the command succeeded. -tab-sep- When successful, this command doesn't return anything. Instead, for each channel, one message with the first element being the string `subscribe` is pushed as a confirmation that the command succeeded. {{< /multitabs >}}
intro:
Subscribes the client to the specified channels.

Once the client enters the subscribed state it is not supposed to issue any
other commands, except for additional `SUBSCRIBE`, [`SSUBSCRIBE`](), [`PSUBSCRIBE`](), [`UNSUBSCRIBE`](), [`SUNSUBSCRIBE`](), 
[`PUNSUBSCRIBE`](), [`PING`](), [`RESET`]() and [`QUIT`]() commands.
However, if RESP3 is used (see [`HELLO`]()) it is possible for a client to issue any commands while in subscribed state.

For more information, see [Pub/sub]().

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Subscribes the client to the specified channels.

Once the client enters the subscribed state it is not supposed to issue any
other commands, except for additional `SUBSCRIBE`, [`SSUBSCRIBE`](), [`PSUBSCRIBE`](), [`UNSUBSCRIBE`](), [`SUNSUBSCRIBE`](), 
[`PUNSUBSCRIBE`](), [`PING`](), [`RESET`]() and [`QUIT`]() commands.
However, if RESP3 is used (see [`HELLO`]()) it is possible for a client to issue any commands while in subscribed state.

For more information, see [Pub/sub]().

sections.title: Behavior change history
sections.content: *   `>= 6.2.0`: [`RESET`]({{< relref "/commands/reset" >}}) can be called to exit subscribed state.

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility
sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | |

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
{{< multitabs id="subscribe-return-info" 
    tab1="RESP2" 
    tab2="RESP3" >}}

When successful, this command doesn't return anything. Instead, for each channel, one message with the first element being the string `subscribe` is pushed as a confirmation that the command succeeded.

-tab-sep-

When successful, this command doesn't return anything. Instead, for each channel, one message with the first element being the string `subscribe` is pushed as a confirmation that the command succeeded.

{{< /multitabs >}}
